description_long: This robot dog features a sleek, angular body with a predominantly white and beige color scheme accented by dark gray and yellow components. Its design includes four articulated limbs, each jointed for mobility, with the front limbs appearing more robust and equipped with mechanical actuators. The head is compact and streamlined, with a protruding sensor or camera module, and the tail is thin and rod-like, possibly serving as an antenna or balance aid. The surface is hard and smooth, with visible seams and mechanical joints at the limb connections, suggesting advanced articulation. Several cylindrical and conical elements are attached to the limbs and body, likely serving as sensors or functional appendages. The overall build is compact and robust, suitable for navigating urban environments and interacting with people or objects.
description: A compact, angular robot dog with articulated limbs, a streamlined head, and a rod-like tail, finished in white, beige, and dark gray with yellow accents.
description_view_0: Front view showing the head with a sensor module, robust front limbs, and the start of the tail.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated body, jointed limbs, and the full length of the rod-like tail.
description_view_2: Top-down view emphasizing the symmetrical limb placement and the compact, angular body shape.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the rear limbs, tail, and additional cylindrical appendages.
